NCT00262600.name: RE-LY
NCT00262600.group: Non-valvular AF, dabigatran 150 mg BID vs dose-adjusted warfarin (open-label PROBE)
NCT00262600.snippet: Source: Connolly SJ et al. NEJM 2009;361:1139-1151 (RE-LY dabigatran vs warfarin).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Connolly SJ et al. NEJM 2009;361:1139-1151, CONSORT (page 1141)
evidence.text: 18,113 patients with non-valvular atrial fibrillation at risk for stroke were randomized (open-label PROBE) to dabigatran etexilate 110 mg BID (n=6,015), dabigatran 150 mg BID (n=6,076), or dose-adjusted warfarin target INR 2.0-3.0 (n=6,022). Median follow-up 2.0 years.

evidence.label: Primary — Stroke or Systemic Embolism
evidence.source: Connolly SJ et al. NEJM 2009, Table 2 (page 1145)
evidence.text: Primary outcome of stroke or systemic embolism occurred in 134 patients (1.11% per year) with dabigatran 150 mg vs 199 patients (1.69% per year) with warfarin; hazard ratio 0.66 (95% CI 0.53 to 0.82; P<0.001 for superiority).

evidence.label: Major Bleeding Safety
evidence.source: Connolly SJ et al. NEJM 2009, Table 3
evidence.text: Major bleeding 3.11% per year with dabigatran 150 mg vs 3.36% per year with warfarin; HR 0.93 (95% CI 0.81-1.07). Haemorrhagic stroke markedly reduced: HR 0.26 (0.14-0.49).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias Assessment (RoB 2.0)
evidence.source: Connolly SJ et al. NEJM 2009; RoB 2.0 design-based evaluation
evidence.text: D1 Randomization LOW — computer-generated centrally-stratified 1:1:1 randomization with CHADS2 score strata. D2 Deviations SOME CONCERNS — open-label PROBE design (prospective randomized open-label blinded endpoint adjudication); however CEC blinded to treatment mitigates. D3 Missing data LOW — >99% follow-up. D4 Outcome measurement LOW — independent blinded CEC adjudication. D5 Selection of reported result LOW — pre-specified primary with SAP lock.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 eligibilities.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria Patients with non-valvular atrial fibrillation (AF), at moderate to high risk of stroke, or systemic embolism with at least one additional risk factor (i.e. previous ischemic stroke, TIA, or systemic embolism, left ventricular dysfunction, age >=75 years, age >=65 with either diabetes mellitus, history of coronary artery disease or hypertension)~Exclusion criteria~1. Prosthetic heart valves requiring anticoagulation per se, or hemodynamically relevant valve disease that is ...

evidence.label: Demographics (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 baseline_measurements.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: Male 3865, Male 3840, Male 3809. NOTE: figures are total-arms aggregate from CT.gov baseline-measurements module; per-arm breakdown available in AACT raw data. RACE caveat: review trial-population racial composition for external-validity considerations.

NCT00403767.name: ROCKET-AF
NCT00403767.group: Non-valvular AF at moderate-high stroke risk, rivaroxaban 20 mg QD vs dose-adjusted warfarin
NCT00403767.snippet: Source: Patel MR et al. NEJM 2011;365:883-891 (ROCKET-AF rivaroxaban vs warfarin).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Patel MR et al. NEJM 2011;365:883-891, CONSORT
evidence.text: 14,264 patients with non-valvular AF at moderate-to-high stroke risk (mean CHADS2 score 3.5) were randomized 1:1 double-blind double-dummy to rivaroxaban 20 mg once daily (n=7,081) or dose-adjusted warfarin target INR 2.0-3.0 (n=7,090). Median follow-up 590 days.

evidence.label: Primary — Stroke or Systemic Embolism
evidence.source: Patel MR et al. NEJM 2011, Figure 2 (page 888)
evidence.text: Primary endpoint of stroke or systemic embolism occurred in 269 rivaroxaban patients (1.7% per year) vs 306 warfarin patients (2.2% per year); hazard ratio 0.88 (95% CI 0.75 to 1.03; P<0.001 for non-inferiority).

evidence.label: Major Bleeding and Intracranial Haemorrhage
evidence.source: Patel MR et al. NEJM 2011, Table 3
evidence.text: Major and clinically relevant non-major bleeding 14.9% rivaroxaban vs 14.5% warfarin; HR 1.03 (95% CI 0.96-1.11; P=0.44). Intracranial haemorrhage was significantly reduced: 0.5% vs 0.7%; HR 0.67 (0.47-0.93; P=0.02).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.source: Patel MR et al. NEJM 2011 + design-based RoB 2.0 evaluation
evidence.text: D1 LOW — central stratified randomization. D2 LOW — double-blind double-dummy placebo control for both INR monitoring and active drug; allocation concealment intact. D3 LOW — <1% lost to follow-up. D4 LOW — independent blinded CEC. D5 LOW — pre-specified SAP with on-treatment primary and ITT secondary.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 eligibilities.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Patients must have documented atrial fibrillation on 2 separate occasions within 6 months before screening~* History of a prior stroke, transient ischemic attack or non-neurologic systemic embolism believed to be cardiac in origin, or at least two of the following risk factors: heart failure, hypertension, age 75 years or greater, diabetes mellitus~Exclusion Criteria:~* Significant mitral stenosis~* Transient atrial fibrillation caused by a reversible disorder~* Active int...

NCT00412984.name: ARISTOTLE
NCT00412984.group: Non-valvular AF, apixaban 5 mg BID vs dose-adjusted warfarin
NCT00412984.snippet: Source: Granger CB et al. NEJM 2011;365:981-992 (ARISTOTLE apixaban vs warfarin).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Granger CB et al. NEJM 2011;365:981-992, CONSORT
evidence.text: 18,201 patients with non-valvular AF and at least one additional stroke risk factor were randomized 1:1 double-blind double-dummy to apixaban 5 mg BID (n=9,120) or dose-adjusted warfarin target INR 2.0-3.0 (n=9,081). Median follow-up 1.8 years.

evidence.label: Primary — Stroke or Systemic Embolism
evidence.source: Granger CB et al. NEJM 2011, Table 2 (page 987)
evidence.text: Primary endpoint of stroke or systemic embolism: 212 apixaban (1.27% per year) vs 265 warfarin (1.60% per year); hazard ratio 0.79 (95% CI 0.66 to 0.95; P=0.01 for superiority). Non-inferiority and superiority both met.

evidence.label: Major Bleeding and Mortality
evidence.source: Granger CB et al. NEJM 2011, Table 3
evidence.text: Major bleeding 2.13% apixaban vs 3.09% warfarin; HR 0.69 (95% CI 0.60-0.80; P<0.001). All-cause mortality 3.52% apixaban vs 3.94% warfarin; HR 0.89 (95% CI 0.80-1.00; P=0.047). Apixaban uniquely shows significant bleeding reduction + mortality reduction.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.source: Granger CB et al. NEJM 2011 + RoB 2.0 evaluation
evidence.text: D1 LOW — central stratified randomization. D2 LOW — double-blind double-dummy. D3 LOW — 98% follow-up. D4 LOW — independent blinded CEC with masked review. D5 LOW — pre-specified primary with SAP; non-inferiority + superiority hierarchical testing.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 eligibilities.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Males and females ≥ 18 yrs with atrial fibrillation (AF) and one or more of the following risk factors for stroke:~* Age ≥ 75, previous stroke~* transient ischemic attack (TIA) or Systemic Embolism (SE)~* Symptomatic congestive heart failure or left ventricular dysfunction with left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) ≤ 40%~* Diabetes mellitus or hypertension requiring pharmacological treatment"

NCT00781391.name: ENGAGE AF-TIMI 48
NCT00781391.group: Non-valvular AF at moderate-high stroke risk, edoxaban 60 mg QD (high-dose) vs dose-adjusted warfarin
NCT00781391.snippet: Source: Giugliano RP et al. NEJM 2013;369:2093-2104 (ENGAGE AF-TIMI 48 edoxaban vs warfarin).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Giugliano RP et al. NEJM 2013;369:2093-2104, CONSORT
evidence.text: 21,105 patients with non-valvular AF at moderate-to-high stroke risk (CHADS2 score >=2) randomized 1:1:1 double-blind double-dummy to high-dose edoxaban 60 mg QD (n=7,035), low-dose edoxaban 30 mg QD (n=7,034), or dose-adjusted warfarin (n=7,036). Median follow-up 2.8 years.

evidence.label: Primary — Stroke or Systemic Embolism
evidence.source: Giugliano RP et al. NEJM 2013, Figure 1
evidence.text: Primary endpoint of stroke or systemic embolism: 296 patients (1.18% per year) with edoxaban 60 mg vs 337 (1.50% per year) with warfarin; hazard ratio 0.79 (95% CI 0.68 to 0.92; P<0.001 for non-inferiority; P=0.08 for superiority on ITT).

evidence.label: Major Bleeding and Mortality
evidence.source: Giugliano RP et al. NEJM 2013, Table 2
evidence.text: Major bleeding 2.75% edoxaban 60 mg vs 3.43% warfarin; HR 0.80 (0.71-0.91; P<0.001). All-cause mortality 3.99% vs 4.35%; HR 0.87 (0.79-0.96; P=0.006). Significant mortality reduction.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.source: Giugliano RP et al. NEJM 2013 + RoB 2.0 evaluation
evidence.text: D1 LOW — computer-generated central randomization. D2 LOW — double-blind double-dummy. D3 LOW — 99% follow-up. D4 LOW — independent blinded CEC. D5 LOW — SAP pre-locked with non-inferiority + superiority hierarchical testing.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.source: AACT 2026-04-12 eligibilities.txt
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 21 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* 21 years of age or older; male or female.~* Able to provide written informed consent.~* History of documented AF within the prior 12 months~* A moderate to high risk of stroke, as defined by CHADS2 index score of at least 2~Exclusion Criteria:~* Transient atrial fibrillation secondary to other reversible disorders~* Subjects with moderate or severe mitral stenosis, unresected atrial myxoma, or a mechanical heart valve~* Subjects with any contraindication for anticoagulant ...

title: RapidMeta Cardiology | Direct Oral Anticoagulants vs Warfarin in Non-Valvular Atrial Fibrillation v1.0
h1: Direct Oral Anticoagulants vs Warfarin \u2014 Patient Summary
